title: China Approves Amgen's Tarlatamab (Imdelltra) for Advanced Small Cell Lung Cancer
summary: Shanghai: Amgen's lung cancer drug tarlatamab has won approval from вЂ‹China's National Medical Products Administration, its development and commercialisation partner BeOne Medicines said on Friday on...

contentHtml:
Shanghai: Amgen's lung cancer drug tarlatamab has won approval from вЂ‹China's National Medical Products Administration, its development and commercialisation partner BeOne Medicines said on Friday on WeChat.

The drug вЂ‹is a targeted immunotherapy вЂ‹for adults in the extensive-stage of вЃ hard-to-treat small cell lung cancer вЂ‹that has worsened despite chemotherapy.

The вЂ‹U.S. drugmaker sells tarlatamab in its home market under the name Imdelltra. It is вЂ‹part of Amgen's pipeline of вЂ‹bispecific antibodies designed to attach to a вЂЊcancer вЃ cell and an immune cell, bringing them together so that the body's immune system can kill вЂ‹the cancer.

Amgen вЂ‹and Hong вЃ Kong-listed BeOne did not respond to requests for вЂ‹comment on launch date вЂ‹or вЃ pricing for the Chinese market.

Some Wall Street analysts have said tarlatamab вЃ could represent вЂ‹an annual sales opportunity вЂ‹for Amgen of more than $2 billion.

Source : Reuters
